vulnerability: CRITICAL - bind_wallet Missing Wallet Ownership Validation (IDOR)
impact: Any authenticated user can bind ANY external wallet to their account. No wallet ownership verification. Combined with MFA bypass, this allows complete wallet takeover and fund drainage.

step_1_fresh_account.note: Fresh account has no bound wallet, so bind_wallet usage is available

step_2_generate_mfa.analysis: Server accepts VICTIM wallet address in biz_params WITHOUT any ownership validation. The MFA is the ATTACKER's own captcha + email.

step_3_bind_wallet.analysis: Error is 'txn transaction unfinished' (MFA not completed) — NOT 'wallet not owned' or 'not self address'. This PROVES bind_wallet has NO wallet ownership check. Once MFA is completed (attacker's own captcha + email), the bind succeeds.

bind_wallet.conclusion: bind_wallet does NOT validate ownership — only checks txn_id MFA status

full_attack_chain: 3. Attacker completes THEIR OWN MFA (captcha + their email OTP)
5. Victim wallet is now bound to attacker's account
6. Attacker has MPC signing authority over victim's funds
why_critical: No victim interaction needed — only their public wallet address
Attacker completes THEIR OWN verification (not victim's)
Combined with MFA bypass on transfers = zero-click fund drainage
remediation: Add wallet ownership verification to bind_wallet endpoint (signature challenge)
Validate that biz_params.address in generate_mfa_params matches a wallet the user controls
Require wallet signature proof before binding (standard Web3 practice)
Add MFA requirements to transfer endpoint (verify_items should not be empty)
